macro_segments.title: Automatic Sanctification of the Tenth: Rava's Position That Designation is Not Required

Supporting Baraita: Ninth Called Tenth, Tenth Left in Pen—Both Get Sanctity Without Walking Out

macro_segments.title: Dispute Regarding the Ninth: Rabbi Shimon's Position vs. the Gemara's Rejection

Reasoning: Eleventh Requires Transfer from Tenth; Ninth Has No Competition and Lower Sanctity

macro_segments.title: Minyan Hara'uy (Fit Counting): Animals Exempted Once the Process Could Have Been Completed

micro_segments.title: Rava's Principle: A Fit Counting Exempts Even if Ma'aser Was Not Actually Taken

Rava's Own Derivation from Verse: The Word Ya'avor (Pass By) Excludes Re-counting
Baraita Supporting Rava: Ten Sheep, Five Counted, One Dies—Counted Ones Exempt from Next Cycle

macro_segments.title: Multiple Doors and Multiple Countings: Scenarios Where the Process Could or Could Not Be Completed

micro_segments.title: Case One: Six Out One Door, Four Out Another, Four Remain—Determining Which Are Exempt

Clarification: Minyan Hara'uy Applies When Theoretically Possible, Even If Not Certain

macro_segments.title: Prohibition Against Selective Counting: All Animals Must Be Included in the Process

micro_segments.title: Case of Fifteen Sheep: Cannot Leave Five Out and Count Only Ten
Proper Procedure: All Animals in Pen, Ten Exit, Tenth Becomes Ma'aser, Rest Combine to Next Cycle
Two Innovations: Include All Animals and the Excluded Five Are Not Exempt from Next Cycle
